commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=73e6c4730b27fdaf280c995016ad06bf20b9d128)
commands.review_summary:
Adds a runtime auth refresh hard-deadline helper, wraps the embedded runner refresh single-flight with it, and adds focused unit/regression tests.

PR surface: Source +56, Tests +112. Total +168 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source: current main awaits refreshInFlight without a timeout, and a never-settling prepareProviderRuntimeAuth leaves every later waiter blocked. I did not run a live gateway repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The contributor needs to fix the auth-state race and provide their own real behavior proof; automation cannot supply external after-fix gateway proof for them.

target.title: fix(agents): hard-deadline runtime auth refresh to prevent gateway deadlock
target.body:
## Summary

A long-running gateway can permanently deadlock its entire agent lane on a stuck OAuth auth-refresh. The runtime auth single-flight in `auth-controller.ts` stores the in-flight refresh promise in `runtimeAuthState.refreshInFlight`; every model turn that needs auth does `await runtimeAuthState.refreshInFlight` **with no timeout**. If the underlying provider auth hook (`prepareProviderRuntimeAuth` -> OAuth refresh, keychain read, or cross-agent file-lock wait) never settles, `refreshInFlight` is never cleared and **every subsequent turn hangs forever** awaiting it. The process stays alive and the port stays open, so it looks healthy while no replies are produced.

Observed in production: a gateway froze mid-day right after a token hit expiry. Process alive (11d uptime, port listening) but fully idle since the freeze, zero sockets to the provider, all threads parked, one never-resolving `await`. On-disk token was valid the whole time (a fresh process pre-warmed auth in ~0.7s), and no on-disk lock was held — i.e. a pure in-memory single-flight deadlock.

This adds a hard deadline so the single-flight promise **always** settles:

- New `withRuntimeAuthRefreshDeadline()` + `RUNTIME_AUTH_REFRESH_HARD_TIMEOUT_MS` (300s) in `runtime-auth-refresh.ts`. 300s sits above the OAuth manager's own 120s call timeout plus a peer's 180s stale-lock window, so a legitimately slow cross-agent refresh still completes while a true hang is forced to reject.
- `refreshRuntimeAuth` wraps the refresh operation in that deadline; on timeout it rejects, the `.finally` clears `refreshInFlight`, and all waiters unblock with a descriptive error instead of hanging.
- The `.finally` clear is no longer gated on `generation`: a profile rotation during a slow/hung refresh must still release the handle it owns, otherwise a stale handle wedges the new generation. The existing `refreshInFlight === refreshPromise` identity guard still prevents clobbering a newer refresh.

No behavior change on the happy path (fast refresh resolves long before the deadline). The deadline is a backstop only.

## Repro / Test plan

- [x] `withRuntimeAuthRefreshDeadline` unit tests: rejects after the deadline when work never settles (fake timers), resolves/propagates when work wins the race, passes through unbounded for non-positive timeout, and the default exceeds the OAuth call + stale-lock budget.
- [x] `auth-controller` regression test: a `prepareProviderRuntimeAuth` that hangs forever sets `refreshInFlight`, then after advancing past the hard deadline the wedged handle rejects with `exceeded hard deadline` and is no longer the active in-flight handle.
- [x] Proof the test catches the bug: with the deadline neutralized (timeout `0` = unbounded), the regression test **times out / FAILS**; restored, it passes.
- [x] `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/runtime-auth-refresh.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/auth-controller.test.ts` -> 17 tests pass.
- [x] `pnpm tsgo` clean for touched files; `scripts/run-oxlint.mjs` clean; `pnpm build` clean (no `[INEFFECTIVE_DYNAMIC_IMPORT]`).

Thanks @amittell.
target.author: amittell
